last_completed_round.hypothesis: Round 12 repaired the search-surface mismatch and restored the PTX microkernel as the dominant hot-band launch. The new best run at 24.16427231 ms slightly improved the exact scheduler-facing counters that still matter on this branch: active warps rose to 16.63%, barrier eased to 5.43%, and long-scoreboard eased to 7.17%, while DRAM stayed low at 12.44%. A source diff against `df5bac2` is now comment-only, so the restore and export-cleanup families are absorbed again and should be filtered out. The highest-value unabsorbed follow-on on the active PTX surface is therefore the narrow handoff seam around `cp_async_wait_group_0`, `__syncthreads()`, and the future-tile refill order. The prior barrier/handoff attempt should not be treated as negative evidence here because it was measured on the non-PTX sibling dispatch, not on the restored PTX microkernel that now owns the runtime.

notes: Build passed for round 14/100. Node A will measure the result next.
